title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The dishwasher has not filled with enough water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error i10 on Electrolux dishwashers indicates the appliance did not detect the required water level within the timeout period. This is typically a water supply problem, from a closed tap to a faulty inlet valve.

causes.cause: Water supply tap turned off or partially closed
Inlet hose kinked, crushed, or blocked
Inlet filter screen clogged with debris

symptoms: Cycle fails to progress past fill stage

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water supply tap is fully open and delivering adequate pressure.

fixSteps.title: Check inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the inlet hose for kinks, bends, or damage. Straighten or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the hose from the valve and clean the mesh filter screen with a small brush.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to check for voltage at the valve during fill. If voltage is present but no water flows, the valve is faulty and needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve needs replacement and you are not comfortable with electrical connections.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i10 water fill error fix

faq.q: What does i10 mean on an Electrolux dishwasher?
faq.a: It means the dishwasher did not fill with enough water in the allowed time.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause i10?
faq.a: Yes, minimum 1 bar pressure is typically required for proper filling.

faq.q: How do I clean the inlet filter?
faq.a: Disconnect the inlet hose from the back of the machine and pull out the mesh screen with pliers. Rinse under running water.

title: Drain Failure
shortDescription: The dishwasher could not drain the water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error i20 on Electrolux dishwashers indicates the drain cycle did not complete. Water remains in the sump area. Usually caused by a blocked filter, kinked drain hose, or a failed drain pump.

causes.cause: Drain filter clogged with food debris
Drain pump impeller jammed by glass or bone
Sink drain or garbage disposal blocked

symptoms: Standing water in the bottom of the tub
Drain pump silent or making strained noise

fixSteps.title: Clean drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom rack, unscrew and remove the filter assembly. Clean all components thoroughly under running water.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for kinks or blockages. Ensure there is a proper high loop or air gap.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the pump housing for foreign objects jamming the impeller. Remove any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump motor has failed, disconnect power, remove the base panel, and swap the pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor has failed and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i20 drain error fix

faq.q: Why is there water sitting in the bottom of my Electrolux dishwasher?
faq.a: The i20 error means the drain system is blocked. Clean the filter and check the drain hose first.

faq.q: How often should I clean the dishwasher filter?
faq.a: At least once a month to prevent i20 drain errors.

faq.q: Could a blocked garbage disposal cause i20?
faq.a: Yes, if the dishwasher drains through the disposal, a blockage there will prevent draining.

title: Flood Protection Activated
shortDescription: The anti-flood system has detected water in the base of the dishwasher.
fullDescription: Error i30 on Electrolux dishwashers triggers when the float switch in the base pan detects water leakage. The machine will stop all operations and activate the drain pump continuously to evacuate water.

fixSteps.title: Tilt and drain
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power, pull the machine out, and tilt it slightly to drain water from the base pan.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the base plate and inspect all hose connections, the door seal, and the sump for signs of leakage.

fixSteps.title: Check float switch
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the float in the base pan moves freely and is not stuck in the raised position.

fixSteps.title: Repair leak source
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten loose connections, replace cracked hoses, or replace the door seal as needed.

whenToCallPro: If the leak source is a cracked sump or internal component.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i30 flood leak error fix

faq.q: Why does my Electrolux dishwasher keep pumping water?
faq.a: The i30 flood protection activates the drain pump continuously to remove leaked water from the base.

faq.q: Is i30 dangerous?
faq.a: The machine is designed to protect against flooding. Investigate the leak promptly to prevent water damage.

faq.q: Can I reset i30 without fixing the leak?
faq.a: You can reset it by draining the base, but the error will return if the leak is not fixed.

title: Pressure Switch Fault
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor has provided an invalid reading.
fullDescription: Error i40 on Electrolux dishwashers indicates the analogue pressure sensor is providing readings outside its expected range. This prevents the machine from properly controlling water fill levels.

causes.cause: Pressure sensor hose disconnected or cracked

fixSteps.title: Inspect pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the base panel and check the small rubber hose connecting the sump to the pressure sensor. Ensure it is firmly attached and not cracked.

fixSteps.title: Clear blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Blow gently through the pressure hose to clear any debris or water.

fixSteps.title: Test pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across the sensor terminals. Compare with specification values from service manual.

fixSteps.title: Replace pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If readings are out of range, replace the pressure sensor module.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor or control board needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i40 pressure sensor error fix

faq.q: What does the pressure sensor do in a dishwasher?
faq.a: It measures the water level so the control board knows when to stop filling.

faq.q: Can i40 cause flooding?
faq.a: Potentially, if the sensor fails to detect the correct level the machine could overfill.

faq.q: Is the pressure sensor expensive?
faq.a: Typically between 20 and 50 dollars for the part itself.

title: Wash Motor Triac Failure
shortDescription: The control board has detected a short circuit on the wash motor triac.
fullDescription: Error i50 on Electrolux dishwashers indicates a short circuit condition on the triac that controls the wash motor. The control board shuts down the motor circuit to prevent damage.

causes.cause: Wash motor triac shorted on control board
Wiring harness damage between board and motor

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring harness between the control board and the wash motor for damage, burns, or loose connectors.

fixSteps.title: Test wash motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor and measure winding resistance. Compare with specifications.

fixSteps.title: Inspect control board
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burn marks or damaged components on the PCB near the motor triac.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the control board or wash motor depending on which has failed.

whenToCallPro: Recommended for this error as it involves electrical components on the control board.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i50 wash motor triac error

faq.q: Can I fix i50 myself?
faq.a: This error usually requires professional repair as it involves control board or motor replacement.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing an i50 error?
faq.a: Compare the repair cost to the machine's age and value. Board plus labor can be expensive on older models.

faq.q: What is a triac?
faq.a: A triac is an electronic component on the control board that switches power to the motor on and off.

title: Heating Element Failure
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected a fault with the water heating system.
fullDescription: Error i60 on Electrolux dishwashers indicates the water is not reaching the target temperature. The heater element, NTC temperature sensor, or heating relay on the control board may be at fault.

causes.cause: Wiring to heater disconnected or damaged
Heating relay on control board failed

symptoms: Dishes greasy or not properly cleaned
Cycle takes much longer than normal
Steam or hot air absent at end of cycle

fixSteps.title: Test heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the machine. Access the heater and measure resistance. It should read between 20 and 30 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the NTC sensor resistance at room temperature (should be around 5k ohms at 25C).

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check all connections from the control board to the heater and NTC sensor for damage or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty part
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the heating element, NTC sensor, or control board as diagnosed.

whenToCallPro: If the control board heating relay has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i60 heating element error fix

faq.q: Why are my dishes greasy after a wash?
faq.a: The i60 error means water is not heating, so grease is not being dissolved properly.

faq.q: How do I test the heating element?
faq.a: Use a multimeter to measure resistance across the element terminals. It should be 20 to 30 ohms.

faq.q: What is the NTC sensor?
faq.a: It is a temperature sensor that tells the control board the current water temperature.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication has been lost between the main control board and the user interface board.
fullDescription: Error iC0 on Electrolux dishwashers indicates a communication breakdown between the main electronic control board and the user interface panel. This prevents the user from controlling the machine.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable between boards loose or damaged
Main control board communication circuit failed
Power supply instability or voltage spike

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect from power for 5 minutes, then reconnect. This can reset communication.

fixSteps.title: Check ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door panel and inspect the flat ribbon cable connecting the UI board to the main board. Reseat both connectors firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Check both boards for signs of moisture or corrosion that could disrupt communication.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating does not resolve the issue, replace the UI board or main board as needed.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the ribbon cable does not fix the issue.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher iC0 communication error fix

faq.q: Can a power outage cause iC0?
faq.a: Yes, power surges or outages can disrupt communication. A power cycle often resolves it.

faq.q: What boards does iC0 involve?
faq.a: The main control board and the user interface panel at the top of the door.

faq.q: Is iC0 expensive to fix?
faq.a: If it is just a loose cable, it is free. Board replacement ranges from 40 to 90 dollars for the part.

title: Water Fill Timing Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher detects an abnormal water fill duration, indicating a partial blockage or sensor drift.
fullDescription: Error iF0 on Electrolux dishwashers indicates the water fill is taking longer than expected but has not fully timed out. This is a softer version of i10 and usually points to restricted flow or sensor calibration issues.
symptoms: Fill cycle takes longer than usual
Cycle completes but with poor wash results

fixSteps.title: Check water pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Verify adequate pressure by running the kitchen tap. Pressure should be above 1 bar.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and thoroughly clean the inlet hose mesh filter.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Check valve opens fully when powered. Listen for restricted flow sounds.

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve does not open fully, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the filter does not resolve the slow fill.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher iF0 slow fill error fix

faq.q: Is iF0 the same as i10?
faq.a: They are related. iF0 indicates slow filling, while i10 means the fill completely timed out.

faq.q: Can iF0 appear and disappear?
faq.a: Yes, it can be intermittent if water pressure fluctuates during the day.

faq.q: Will iF0 damage my dishwasher?
faq.a: No direct damage, but poor fill levels result in poor cleaning performance.

title: Fill Switch Fault
shortDescription: The fill level switch is reporting an incorrect or stuck signal.
fullDescription: Error i11 indicates the water level switch is stuck in the full position even when the tub is empty, or it remains in the empty position when water is present. The control board cannot trust the level readings.

causes.cause: Fill switch contacts stuck or corroded

fixSteps.title: Clean pressure chamber
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the pressure chamber at the base. Clear any debris that may be giving false readings.

fixSteps.title: Test fill switch
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the fill switch contacts for continuity. Check both states.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wiring harness from the switch to the control board for breaks or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace fill switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch is faulty, replace it with the correct part.

whenToCallPro: If the control board is suspected to be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i11 fill switch error

faq.q: What is the fill switch?
faq.a: A sensor that detects whether the tub has reached the correct water level.

faq.q: Can cleaning fix i11?
faq.a: Yes, if the pressure chamber is blocked, cleaning it can restore correct readings.

faq.q: Is i11 related to i10?
faq.a: They are related. i10 is a fill timeout while i11 is a faulty switch reading.

title: Drain Timeout with Sensor Error
shortDescription: The drain cycle timed out and the water level sensor still detects water.
fullDescription: Error i21 is a variant of i20 where the drain has timed out and the pressure sensor confirms water is still present. This double confirmation points to a genuine drain obstruction rather than a sensor fault.
symptoms: Pump runs but water does not decrease

fixSteps.title: Remove standing water
fixSteps.instruction: Manually bail out standing water from the tub to access the filter.

fixSteps.title: Deep clean filter and sump
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and thoroughly clean the entire filter assembly and sump area.

fixSteps.title: Check drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump from below and check for major blockages. Clear any debris from the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump impeller is damaged or the motor is seized, replace the entire pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the pump motor is seized and requires replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i21 drain error pump blocked

faq.q: How is i21 different from i20?
faq.a: i21 confirms water is still present via the sensor, making it a more definitive drain failure than i20.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher with i21 showing?
faq.a: No, the machine will not operate until the drain issue is resolved.

faq.q: What commonly blocks the drain pump?
faq.a: Glass shards, small bones, fruit pits, and broken dish fragments are common culprits.

title: Continuous Flood Detection
shortDescription: The flood protection system has been triggered repeatedly, indicating an ongoing leak.
fullDescription: Error i31 is a persistent version of i30. It means the machine has detected water in the base pan multiple times, suggesting the leak source has not been resolved. The machine locks out to prevent water damage.
symptoms: Machine locked out and will not start
Repeated i30 errors before i31 appears

fixSteps.title: Dry the base pan completely
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the machine out, tilt to drain, and use towels to dry the base pan thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Run a short cycle and observe
fixSteps.instruction: With the base plate removed, run a short cycle and watch for the leak source.

fixSteps.title: Repair the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten connections, replace the door seal, or patch the leaking component as identified.

fixSteps.title: Reset flood protection
fixSteps.instruction: After repair, dry the base pan, ensure the float switch returns to the down position, and power cycle.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot identify the leak source after visual inspection.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i31 persistent leak flood error

faq.q: Why did i31 appear instead of i30?
faq.a: i31 means the flood sensor has been triggered multiple times, indicating an unresolved leak.

faq.q: Is i31 a lockout error?
faq.a: Yes, the machine will not operate until the base pan is dry and the leak is fixed.

faq.q: Can I override the flood protection?
faq.a: No, and you should not attempt to. The leak must be found and repaired first.

title: Wash Motor Triac Open Circuit
shortDescription: The control board detects the wash motor triac is not conducting when it should be.
fullDescription: Error i51 is the complement of i50. While i50 indicates a short circuit on the motor triac, i51 indicates the triac is open and not passing current to the wash motor. The motor cannot run.

causes.cause: Wash motor triac open circuit on control board
Wiring disconnect between board and motor

symptoms: Dishes come out exactly as loaded
Error appears immediately when cycle begins

fixSteps.title: Check motor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and inspect the motor plug connector. Ensure it is firmly seated and not corroded.

fixSteps.title: Test motor continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor and check winding resistance with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Check thermal cutout
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor has a thermal cutout, test it for continuity. It may have tripped due to overheating.

fixSteps.title: Replace board or motor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the control board if the triac is faulty, or the motor if the winding is open.

whenToCallPro: Recommended for this error due to control board diagnostics.
videoSearchQuery: Electrolux dishwasher i51 wash motor open circuit error

faq.q: Is i51 the same as i50?
faq.a: No. i50 is a short circuit on the motor triac while i51 is an open circuit. Both prevent the motor from running.

faq.q: Can a thermal cutout cause i51?
faq.a: Yes, if the motor overheated and the cutout tripped, the circuit appears open.

faq.q: Should I replace the board or the motor?
faq.a: Diagnostics with a multimeter will determine which component has failed.
